No worries.

I'm doing well.

So yeah, for sure.
George isn't able to make the call today.

Are all able to take a look at the new proposal for kind of like the slower, the slower ramp up?
Yeah, I think we want to go ahead with it.

I just need to like go to finance and speak with them.
Am I understanding correctly that we would do this for like two months or something or three months?
And then it would, and then we would like reduce back down.

The idea is that we, I think we could re-evaluate sort of after, after about three months, like, and at that point, depending on the results that we're seeing, we could either continue doing the 15 a week after we've like fully ramped up to that, or we could sort of re-evaluate.
And then at that point, like, depending on what we actually decide, we could also like alter it, alter the contract in that way.
But I think-
Yeah, the idea is just that there's so much that we could capture right now that it's worth sort of sprinting to get there.
And then at that point, yeah, again, depending on the performance, we can always go back to the, I think the originally proposed was 10 a week versus the 15 a week.
Okay, cool.
Let's go ahead for January and February, and then we can see where we are towards the end of February and make some from there.
Perfect.
Awesome.
That sounds great.
Cool.
And then on the topic of, yeah, just like the quiet week that we were talking about.
So yeah, Will has already, and I think you already saw the Sam, Will has already gone on ahead and written some of the articles for that we can publish during quiet week.
So we'll be good to continue to continue publishing through that.
And then I'm thinking first week of January.
can kind of start with like the week one outlined in the scaling proposal plan.
And so at that point, we would go to the two articles per day beginning in January.
Okay, cool.
So from the 5th of January, we'll move to the new plan.
Yeah, exactly.
Exactly.
Okay.
All right.
That sounds good.
Perfect.
Perfect.
Another action item.
So I, yeah, I was looking at the performance in Looker, and I think it looks like I've lost access to GA4.
Oh, yeah.
Sorry.
There was a problem on the weekend that caused everyone to lose access to everything.
Okay.
For sure.
Yeah.
Okay.
Yeah.
So if either I could get access back, that'd be great.
Or I also gave you editing access to the Looker dashboard.

versions, but now those charts are just broken because the data source isn't there anymore.

Okay, great.
Awesome.
Perfect, perfect.
Okay, cool.

And then we're, you know, of course, still checking in async via Slack.
But then just looking at that, like, two-week performance cadence, I think, is maybe a little bit more helpful than just, like, the week over week.
And that way we're not getting, like, I don't know, that way we're not, like, overreacting to, like, very small changes.
Yeah, could we, would you mind if we did that from February?
I just want to make sure that, like, as we ramp up the content, we, like, we're staying really close to what's going on.
For sure.
Yeah, we can do that starting in February.
Okay, great.
Sounds good.
Cool.
Let's see.

Yep.

Impressions went up a lot.
Clicks went up.
And that was specifically for guides.
And then the top page has consistently been best AI app builders in 2025.

So I think that would be even just a good candidate to just like very quickly shift.
If like January 1st, we're saying, okay, 2026 now, that's a very easy, easy change.
Megan, will you, will GrowthX manage, like, updating all of those?
Yeah, I think we, we can.
I think it's, I need to check because I feel like the publishing flow right now is that we have to file a pull request to make changes to the blog.
So I think we'll just have to, we can do another, like, we can, we can do a pull request for the blog and then kind of go from there, just how we normally, normally do it.
And actually, yeah, yeah, that reminds me.
So Rachel, at one point, she was talking about potentially changing the, the, the CMS that Lovable uses.
I was wondering if, like, you had any, any updates around that, just because, like, ideally, we wouldn't have to file a PR to publish content on the blog.

But, and I think, like, there was also talk about, like, even just, like, having, like, a, like, a publishing bot just to kind of, like, reduce some of that, some of those, like, steps in that process.
Yes.
So, I mean, there's a lot of discussion back and forth here exactly how we're going to do this.
guess when you refer to the blog, you're actually referring to those, like, slash guides, right?
Sorry, guides.
That actually has a CMS.
Not an optimal CMS either, but, so, currently, we're going to experiment with a flow that is sort of integrating cloud code into Slack in a way that could enable easier publishing.
So, we're going to try that first.
And I doubt, actually, that we're going to go to a traditional CMS.
We're going to solve it somehow.
Now, there's also a path that we're investigating to actually build a CMS unlovable and then publish from that.
Yeah, that'd be cool.
So, but we'll keep...
We posted when we have a more updated flow here, we're aware that it's not ideal.
Yeah.
And to that end, we have also noticed some things in the sort of guides that we have published that we could also discuss, like some meta tag stuff.
Yeah.
But I guess we don't want to repeat for the future.
I don't know exactly if it's you who sets that or if it's us, but yeah.
For the meta tags?
Yeah, exactly.
For the meta title on many of these are just, it's just lovable.
Okay.
Yeah, that's weird.
There's something about that, but I don't know if that's just old ones for, or if it's all the ones that are also published now.
So that's, we're going to have to have a look at that, but we're retroactively sort of changing all the, the ones that exist, but just so we make sure that.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
It actually didn't affect the guide, but let me come back to you on this, because I think it did affect, now that I think about it, it did affect the templates, but not the guides.
Okay, okay.
Yeah, because it looks like there are proper meta, like, titles and descriptions for the guides.
Oh, yeah, but now I see what you're saying, yeah, for the templates, it just says, Lovable, yeah, okay.
Okay, I'll still flag that for our engineering team, and see if they're aware of that, if there's anything we can do on our end.
Yeah, it was a problem that also appears on some other pages on our website, so I don't know.
okay.
Like, has to do, yeah, I'm still digging a little bit into this, but just wanted to mention it.
But we will keep you in the loop either way when we have a new sort of publishing solution, but for now, this is what we do.
All good, all good, okay, sounds good.
Okay, great.
Yeah, I mean, those were the main updates that I had, but was there anything that you all wanted to discuss just while we're overall here?
Or flag or anything?
I guess for me, it's one other topic.
I don't know about you, Sam.
We discussed also from the SEO side to build out a number of content clusters around topics that we want to be associated with and want to build authority around, let's say.
And I'm, I guess I'm going to define a number of clusters and we're going to see how we execute on that.
But that's something that I think that we like discussed to not just, I guess, chase individual like SEO, like, what do you say?
Phrases?
But rather, say like, well, safe vibe coding for enterprises is like an area that we want to be associated with and want to build.
content around, and then we should build that, like, kind of pillar and cluster pages, and sort of build authority around a certain number of areas.
Yeah, absolutely.
And so, but you're saying that that's something that you're sort of working on, kind of determining what those, sort of what those, like, areas are that you want to, okay, great, yeah.
Exactly, and we will see, like, how we execute on that, whether that's something you can also sort of build on with this, like, effort, or, like, how exactly that's going to be shaped.
But I thought I would just mention it, because it might be so that we want to direct some of the content to support that effort.
Absolutely.
Yeah, yeah, no, for sure.

Yeah, we definitely, like, yeah, because we already have some, like, general topic clusters of, like, but it's, it's, it's a little more general than even just, like, what you've said of, like, Vibe Coding for Enterprise.
It's, like, right now, it's, like, app comparisons, or, like, business and app ideas.
Or like competitive comparison.
So I think it's definitely worthwhile to get more specific and granular.
And then like we already have a lot of keywords.

And so then I think at that point, once we get that list from you in terms of like the clusters that we want to target, then we can see, okay, like of the keywords that we've already identified, which of these actually fit into these areas.
And if they don't fit into those areas, then we can scrap them and then just do additional research to build out those pillars and those clusters.
Yep.
Sounds good.
Cool.
Cool.
Yeah, Sam, anything else that, anything else from you?
I think from my side.
Okay.
Okay, great.
And then in terms of, yeah, what, when would you like to meet next?
I know, yeah, holidays can be tricky.
Should we do the week of the 5th of June?
Yeah, that sounds, that sounds good.
Yeah, and I think, so y'all, I think typically, like, the, I guess it's like the 8.30 Pacific is like your ideal, maybe.
Sorry, let me, let me quickly see what time that is.
It's, which I think.
Is it 8.45 Pacific right now?
It's 9.45 Pacific right now.
Okay, cool.
And, Johanna, when do you need to be offline in the afternoons?
It's, like, I don't pick up my kid every day, but I guess the, the sort of critical window is sort of between 5 and 7.30 over here.
So I would kind of prefer to do, but of course that's maybe not ideal for you, Sam, but I would prefer to do, like, after.
after.
7.30, or before 5.00, that's pretty early for you guys, I assume.
So if we did 7.30 Central Eastern Time one day, that would be fine.
Which is, I think, is an hour later than this slot today.
So that would be 9.30, oh, sorry, that would be 10.30 in Pacific.
Okay, okay, great, thanks for handling all those time zone conversions.
Yeah, I I trying to get in my head.
Okay, great, so, okay, so 7.30 Central Eastern Europe time zone is what we're looking at.
And then, any day of, like, do you want to do, like, the Monday or Tuesday?
Monday or Tuesday works best for me.
Okay, okay, great, cool.
Then I'll just, yeah, I'll just sync with George offline and then I'll send over a calendar invitation for whichever one of those works, works best.
Yeah.
Cool.
Yeah.
And yeah, in the meantime, like I will be like, I'm not really doing much for the holidays.
I'm going to try and like take, I'm going to try and like keep it low key, but I'll still be like checking Slack like every day just in case anything comes up between now and when we next chat.
But, but yeah, we'll be able to get all of the, we'll continue communicating in Slack just around the timing of publishing those articles for quiet week.
And yeah, so just, yeah, just if anything comes up, if you just like at me in Slack, then I'll, I'll get the push notification.
So.
Cool.
Cool.
All right.
Well, thanks so much, everyone.
Yeah.
And yeah, really happy holidays to the both of you.
And I hope you're able to get some rest and also just, yeah, excited about the scaling.
Oh yeah.
And of course, congrats.
Congratulations on the big funding round, I'm it's an exciting time, so, yeah.
Cool, thanks Megan.
All right, yeah, thanks everyone.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize the content scaling plan and align on operational details.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=55.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Content Scaling Approved:** The plan to increase content production to 15 articles/week is approved for Jan–Feb 2025. This accelerates growth while the market opportunity is high; performance will be re-evaluated at the end of February.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=55.0)
  - [**Bi-Weekly Syncs from Feb:** Syncs will shift from weekly to bi-weekly starting in February 2025. This provides a more meaningful performance review cadence, avoiding overreaction to minor week-over-week fluctuations.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=270.0)
  - [**SEO Strategy Shift:** The strategy will move from targeting individual keywords to building authority around specific, granular content clusters (e.g., "safe vibe coding for enterprises"). Johanna will define these clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
  - [**Critical Blockers Identified:** GA4 access was lost, breaking Looker dashboards and blocking performance analysis. A meta tag bug is also affecting SEO for template pages.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)

## Topics

### Content Scaling Plan

  - [**Decision:** Approved the proposal to scale content production to 15 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=55.0)
  - [**Rationale:** To capitalize on a high-opportunity market window.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=120.0)
  - [**Timeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
      - [**Jan–Feb 2025:** 15 articles/week.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=142.0)
      - [**End of Feb 2025:** Re-evaluate performance to decide whether to continue at 15/week or revert to the original 10/week plan.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=83.0)
  - [**Budget:** Sam has internal approval and will finalize with finance.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)

### Operational Blockers & Solutions

  - [**GA4 Access:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
      - [**Problem:** Access was lost during a weekend incident, breaking Looker dashboards and blocking performance analysis.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
      - [**Solution:** Megan will provide the required access level and email address; Sam will restore access ASAP.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [**Meta Tag Bug:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
      - [**Problem:** Template pages have a generic meta title ("Lovable") instead of a descriptive one, which negatively impacts SEO.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=547.0)
      - [**Context:** This is a known, site-wide issue Lovable is investigating.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
      - [**Solution:** Megan will flag the issue with the GrowthX engineering team.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
  - [**Publishing Workflow:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)
      - [**Problem:** The current workflow requires a pull request (PR) for every change, creating a bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=421.0)
      - [**Context:** Lovable is actively exploring alternatives to the current CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)
      - [**Solutions Under Investigation:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)
          - [A Cloud Code integration with Slack for easier publishing.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=493.0)
          - [Building a custom CMS within Lovable.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=525.0)

### Performance & Content Refresh

  - [**Performance (Dec 1–16):**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=336.0)
      - [Impressions and clicks for "guides" increased significantly from Week 49 to Week 50.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)
      - [The top-performing page is "best AI app builders in 2025."](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
  - [**Content Refresh:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
      - [**Action:** Update the top-performing page to "best AI app builders in 2026."](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
      - [**Rationale:** LLMs and Google favor recent content.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)
      - [**Process:** GrowthX will manage the update via a PR.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=421.0)

### SEO Strategy Shift

  - [**New Strategy:** Shift from targeting individual keywords to building authority around specific content clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
  - [**Rationale:** To establish Lovable as a thought leader in key areas (e.g., "safe vibe coding for enterprises").](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
  - [**Ownership:** Johanna will define the initial list of clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
  - [**Execution:** GrowthX will map existing keywords to the new clusters and conduct new research to fill gaps.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=786.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Sam:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)
      - [Finalize the increased budget with finance.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=67.0)
      - [Restore GA4 access for Megan.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
  - [**Megan:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
      - [Provide the required GA4 access details to Sam.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=203.0)
      - [Flag the meta tag bug with the GrowthX engineering team.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=622.0)
      - [Send a calendar invite for the next sync (Jan 5 or 6, 7:30 PM CET).](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=828.0)
  - [**Johanna:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
      - [Define and share the list of new content clusters.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=675.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=156.0)
      - [Manage publishing of "quiet week" articles.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=156.0)
      - [Begin the 15 articles/week plan on Jan 5, 2025.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=176.0)
      - [Update the "best AI app builders" page to "2026" via a PR.](https://fathom.video/share/hp2qtrrz-_yxAdwFPCtyuuXtXTQpKyBG?tab=summary&timestamp=386.0)

action_items.description: Clear budget increase w/ Finance; confirm go-ahead to Megan
Publish 2 articles/day from Jan 5 per scaling plan
Post required GA4 access level + email in Slack; then Sam grant access
Submit PR to update 'Best AI app builders' to 2026; then Sam/Johanna approve
Flag templates meta-title issue to GrowthX engineering; then report back to Johanna
Send SEO content cluster list to Megan; then Megan map keywords + research
Sync w/ George; send Jan 5–6 7:30 CET invite to Sam + Johanna
